items.title: DGCA tightens rules for foreign airlines operating in country
items.desc: India News: NEW DELHI:  India has tightened rules for foreign airlines operating to and from the country. Now, the DGCA can \"revoke or suspend\" a foreign airline'.

items.title: Chernobyl Wildlife Forty Years On
items.desc: It's 40 years since the Chernobyl disaster. This is what it has meant for wildlife living around the devastated nuclear power plant.

items.title: NBA probes Nikola Jokic altercation as Denver Nuggets face pressure vs Minnesota Timberwolves
items.desc: The NBA is investigating a post-game altercation between the Denver Nuggets and Minnesota Timberwolves following Game 4. Nikola Jokic and Jaden McDaniels were involved in a physical exchange after a late basket, leading to league review. Decisions on potentia…

items.title: “Mercury shoots to 44.1°C; experts cite heat dome as main cause
items.desc: Kanpur heatwave: temperature hits 44.1°C as IMD warns of further rise; experts blame a heat dome trapping hot air, pushing heat index near 50°C.

items.title: Patel Properties, GTB Warriors register easy wins
items.desc: Patel Properties beat Crazy Rangers by 49 runs and GTB Warriors thrashed Knight Scorchers by 84 in Cawnpore Cricket T20 League; Campus IIT won KDMA match.

items.title: Fire beaks out in mobile parts warehouse, none injured
items.desc: Major fire hit a mobile parts warehouse in Kanpur’s Sagar Electronics Market on Birhana Road; no injuries reported, short circuit suspected, probe underway.

items.title: Cheating racket busted in UP Home Guard recruitment exam in Kanpur
items.desc: UP Home Guard recruitment exam: Kanpur police bust AI-assisted cheating racket at BNSD Inter College, arrest chemistry lecturer and two others.

items.title: Student beaten with belt and bat in City while appearing for exam
items.desc: Kanpur: Student attacked with belt and bat while appearing for exam in Chakeri area; police investigating complaint and searching for absconding accused.

items.title: Grand passing out parade of trainee constables organised at Police Lines
items.desc: Kanpur Police Lines hosted a passing-out parade for 585 trainee constables, reviewed by Police Commissioner Raghubir Lal; awards were presented to top trainees.

items.title: Marathi rule: Auto drivers may stay off roads today as unions meet
items.desc: Mumbai: The city's s autorickshaw sector is bracing for a fresh flashpoint over the Maharashtra govt's proposal to make working knowledge of Marathi m.

items.title: HC backs hsg soc, allows new builder after 13-year delay
items.desc: Mumbai: The Bombay High Court on Friday declined a builder's plea to restrain a Wadala society from replacing him to redevelop 46 buildings with 900 t.

items.title: Maharashtra: Fire at Kalyan dumping ground, locals fume
items.desc: Kalyan: A major fire broke out at Adharwadi dumping ground in Kalyan on Sunday evening, causing thick smoke to spread across nearby residential areas,.

items.title: A kippa with Israel and Palestine flags got me detained. This is why I wear it
items.desc: Long before the Israeli police detained me last week, my special skullcap had received strong reactions from Palestinians and struck up meaningful conversations with right wingers. It's got to be doing something right

items.title: RPF constables help woman deliver twins at Subedarganj stn
items.desc: RPF women constables at Subedarganj station, Prayagraj, helped a passenger deliver twins on platform 6; mother and babies shifted to hospital.

items.title: Woman’s body found hanging from tree in Pratapgarh village
items.desc: 20-year-old woman found hanging from a tree near her home in Kakraha Miriya village, Pratapgarh; police suspect murder, post-mortem ordered.

items.title: Criminal injured in encounter, arrested
items.desc: Prayagraj: Wanted criminal Dharamveer Lodhi, with 24+ cases and a Rs 25,000 reward, was injured in a Fatehpur police encounter and arrested; pistol, bike seized.

items.title: 4 auto lifters held, 3 bikes recovered
items.desc: Prayagraj police arrested four auto lifters, recovered three stolen bikes and a dismantled one; Civil Lines police held three for crude bomb attack near eatery.

items.title: 45.7℃: Prayagraj 2nd in UP on heat chart
items.desc: Prayagraj records 45.7°C, second highest in UP after Banda’s 46.6°C, as heatwave persists; western disturbance may bring slight relief next week.

items.desc:
